Interface EventDispatcher

All Superinterfaces:
AutoCloseable, NonThrowingAutoCloseable
All Known Implementing Classes:
DefaultEventDispatcher
public interface EventDispatcher extends NonThrowingAutoCloseable
An event dispatcher that can be used to fire/listen to events.

Implementations are not required to throw a NullPointerException if a null reference was passed to any method; they are allowed to silently ignore it and/or just log that a null reference was passed.

Some implementations might need to perform cleanup when they're no longer needed, so this interface extends NonThrowingAutoCloseable.

Warning: event listeners should not fire the same event they received, or events that cause the same event to be re-fired. This can (and will in most cases) lead to infinite loops and unpredictable behaviour. Implementations may try to prevent re-firing the same event, but are NOT required to.

  • Method Details

    • addListener

      <E extends Event> ListenerRemover addListener(Class<E> eventClass, Consumer<? super E> listener)
      Adds a listener for event of the given type or any of its subclasses.

      If the given event listener was already present, then its priority is set to default value.

      Parameters:
      eventClass - the type of the event
      listener - the listener to add
      Returns:
      an object that can be used to remove the listener
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if any argument is null (optional)

    • addListener

      <E extends Event> ListenerRemover addListener(Class<E> eventClass, byte priority, Consumer<? super E> listener)
      Adds a listener for event of the given type or any of its subclasses.

      If the given event listener was already present, then its priority is set to the given one.

      Parameters:
      eventClass - the type of the event
      priority - the priority of the listener
      listener - the listener to add
      Returns:
      an object that can be used to remove the listener
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if any argument is null (optional)
      See Also:
      • MAX_PRIORITY
      • MIN_PRIORITY

    • register

      ListenerRemover register(Object o)
      Registers all instance methods annotated with @EventListener of the given object's class as event listeners. Or, if the given object is a Class, all its static methods annotated with @EventListener are registered as listeners.
      Parameters:
      o - the object
      Returns:
      an object that can be used to remove the listeners
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if a method annotated with @EventListener has invalid arguments
      InaccessibleObjectException - if a method could not be reflectively accessed for any reason
      NullPointerException - if the object is null (optional)
      See Also:
      • EventListener

    • hasListenersFor

      boolean hasListenersFor(Class<? extends Event> eventClass)
      Determines whether there are registered event listeners that listen for the given event class or superclass. This method can be used to determine whether firing an event has any effect. Useful to avoid firing an event that is fired a lot of times (like MouseCursorMovedEvent), thus optimizing the CPU usage.
      Parameters:
      eventClass - the event class to check for
      Returns:
      true if there are any registered listeners that listen for the given event class (or superclass), false otherwise
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the given class object is null (optional)

    • fireEvent

      CompletableFuture<Void> fireEvent(Event event)
      Fires the given event, i.e. activates all listeners that listen to events of the given event's type or supertypes, returning immediately.

      Be aware that listeners may change the mutable state of the given event.

      Parameters:
      event - the event to fire
      Returns:
      a CompletableFuture that completes when all event handlers are done
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the given event is null (optional)

    • fireEventAndWait

      default void fireEventAndWait(Event event)
      Fires the given event, i.e. activates all listeners that listen to events of the given event's type or supertypes, and waits until all event listeners are executed.

      Be aware that listeners may change the mutable state of the given event.

      Parameters:
      event - the event to fire
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the given event is null (optional)

    • newDefault

      static EventDispatcher newDefault()
      Returns a new default implementation of EventDispatcher.
      Returns:
      a new EventDispatcher

    • close

      default void close()
      This method is not expected to throw any checked exceptions.
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
      Specified by:
      close in interface NonThrowingAutoCloseable
      "Implementation Note:"
      By default, this method does nothing. An event dispatcher doesn't usually have closeable resources.
